user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, and my phone number is **555-123-2002**.

For the last few hours I **can’t send picture messages (MMS)** in my messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but when I try to send a photo it just won’t go through. I’m also **currently in France**, so I’m worried I’ve done something wrong with the phone.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
Roaming enabled successfully

{"message":"Thanks, John—found your account and the line for **555-123-2002** is **Active**, but **international roaming was not enabled** on the line. Since you’re in **France**, that can prevent MMS (picture messages) from sending. I’ve now **enabled roaming on your line at no cost**.

Next, on your phone in France:
1) Turn **Data Roaming ON** (phone setting).  
2) Make sure **Mobile Data** is ON.  
3) **Reboot** your phone, then try sending a picture message again.

If it still won’t send after the reboot, tell me what you see when you check your phone’s network status (signal strength + network type like 3G/4G/5G, and whether data roaming shows ON)."}

Data Roaming is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Mobile Data is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: missing
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: No
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay… I tried to follow that, but I’m a bit confused and now I’m worried something’s wrong.

When I went to turn **Data Roaming ON**, it looks like it’s still **OFF**, and **Mobile Data is also OFF**. I did the **reboot** like you said (I was nervous about my photos, but it restarted).

After that, I checked the phone’s network status and it shows:

- **Airplane Mode:** OFF  
- **SIM Card Status:** **missing**  
- **Cellular Connection:** no service / **no signal**  
- **Network type:** none  
- **Mobile Data Enabled:** No  
- **Data Roaming Enabled:** No  
- **Wi‑Fi:** OFF / not connected

And the top of the screen just shows **No Signal** and **Data Disabled** (battery 80%).

Also, I still **cannot send MMS**.

What does it mean that the SIM is “missing”? Did I break something? What do you want me to do next—should I try taking the SIM out and putting it back in?

{"device_id": "D1002", "device_type": "phone", "model": "Smartphone Pro Max", "imei": "234567890123456", "is_esim_capable": true, "activated": true, "activation_date": "2025-02-15 11:45:00", "last_esim_transfer_date": "2025-01-20 09:30:00"}

{"message":"“SIM: missing” usually means the phone isn’t detecting a SIM/eSIM profile right now—so it won’t get cellular service, and MMS won’t work.

Yes—please **try reseating the SIM** (taking it out and putting it back in). This is the correct next step.

Do this:
1) **Power the phone OFF**.
2) Remove the SIM tray, take the SIM out, and **put it back in firmly** (make sure it sits flat in the tray), then reinsert the tray fully.
3) Power the phone back ON.
4) Re-check your phone’s SIM status.

Then tell me what it shows for **SIM Card Status** (Active / Missing / Locked).

If your phone is using **eSIM** (your device supports eSIM), let me know whether you *have a physical SIM card* to reseat—because if you don’t, we’ll take a different path."}

SIM card re-seated successfully.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
Your SIM card is active and working.

I did it — I turned the phone off, took the little SIM tray out, put the SIM back in, and turned it on again (my hands were shaking a bit, honestly).

Now it’s showing:

- **SIM Card Status:** **Active** (it says it’s working)
- And at the top I can see **strong signal** and it says **5G**, but it still says **Data Disabled**.

What should I do next? Do I turn **Mobile Data** back on now (and then Data Roaming)?

{"message":"Yes—now that the SIM is **Active** and you have **5G signal**, the next step is to turn data back on.

Please do this in order:
1) Turn **Mobile Data ON**.
2) Turn **Data Roaming ON** (since you’re in France).
3) Wait ~30 seconds.
4) Try sending a picture (MMS) again.

After you do that, tell me what your network status shows for:
- **Mobile Data Enabled** (Yes/No)
- **Data Roaming Enabled** (Yes/No)
- And whether the MMS send succeeds or gives an error."}

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: Yes
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I did those steps:

- I turned **Mobile Data ON** (it now says **Data Enabled**)
- Then I turned **Data Roaming ON**
- Waited about half a minute

When I check the network status now it shows:

- **Mobile Data Enabled:** **Yes**  
- **Data Roaming Enabled:** **Yes**  
- **Signal:** excellent, **connected**, **5G**  
- Wi‑Fi is off (not connected)

But when I try to send a picture message, it still **won’t send** — the phone says my messaging app **cannot send MMS**.

I’m getting a little frustrated now… I thought turning roaming on would fix it. What else could it be?
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "8.7", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

{"message":"You did the right steps—your line now has signal, **mobile data + roaming are ON**, and you’re on **5G**. Also, your plan data isn’t exhausted (you’ve used **8.7GB of 15GB** this cycle), so this isn’t a “hit the data limit” issue.

At this point, the most common remaining cause for “cannot send MMS” is the phone’s **MMS/APN (MMSC) settings** or a device-side feature interfering.

Please do these checks/actions on your phone:
1) Check your **APN settings** (specifically whether an **MMSC URL** is present).  
2) If anything looks missing/blank/incorrect, **Reset APN settings to default**.  
3) **Reboot the phone** once after resetting APN.
4) If you have **Wi‑Fi Calling ON**, temporarily turn **Wi‑Fi Calling OFF**, then try MMS again.

After you do #1, tell me what your phone shows for:
- **APN name**
- **MMSC URL**

(That will let me confirm whether APN/MMSC is the blocker.)"}

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.

I checked, and (I’m copying what it shows because I don’t really understand it):

- **APN name:** `internet`  
- **MMSC URL:** `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

Also I checked **Wi‑Fi Calling**, and it’s already **OFF**.

Do you still want me to do the **Reset APN settings** and then **reboot**? I just get nervous rebooting and changing settings because I don’t want to lose anything.

{"message":"Yes—please go ahead and **Reset APN settings**, then **reboot**.

To reassure you:
- **Resetting APN** only restores the phone’s *cellular network settings* (how it connects for data/MMS). It **does not delete photos, messages, or apps**.
- A normal **reboot** also **does not erase** your photos or messages.

Please do this sequence:
1) **Reset APN settings** to default.
2) **Reboot** the phone.
3) After it turns back on, confirm **Mobile Data = ON** and **Data Roaming = ON**.
4) Try sending an MMS again.

If it still fails after that, tell me:
- Do you have any **VPN** turned on? (If yes, turn it off and re-test.)
- Is **Data Saver** on? (If yes, turn it off and re-test.)
- What does your phone’s **“can_send_mms”** check/report say (if your phone has that diagnostic), and any exact error text you see when the MMS fails."}

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: Yes
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
VPN is turned OFF.
Data Saver mode is OFF.
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I went ahead and did it:

1) **Reset APN settings** (it said it would reset at reboot)  
2) **Rebooted** the phone

After it came back on, I checked and it shows:

- **Mobile Data:** **Yes (ON)**
- **Data Roaming:** **Yes (ON)**
- **Signal:** excellent, connected, **5G**
- **VPN:** **OFF**
- **Data Saver:** **OFF**

But it still says my messaging app **cannot send MMS messages**.

I’m honestly getting pretty frustrated now because we’ve done a lot already and it still won’t send. What’s the next thing you want me to try?

{"message":"Given what you’ve confirmed (SIM active, strong 5G, mobile data + roaming ON, VPN OFF, Data Saver OFF, APN reset), the next most likely blocker is the **messaging app itself**—specifically **permissions**.

Please check this next:
1) Confirm **mobile data actually works**: open a website (with Wi‑Fi still OFF) and verify it loads.
2) Check your **Messaging app permissions** and make sure it’s allowed:
   - **SMS** permission
   - **Storage/Photos** permission
   If either is missing/denied, enable it.
3) Try sending an MMS again.

Reply with:
- Whether a web page loads over mobile data (Yes/No)
- Whether the Messaging app has **SMS** and **Storage/Photos** permissions granted (Yes/No for each)

If mobile data works and both permissions are granted but MMS still fails, I’ll need to **transfer you to a human agent** because we’ll be past the fixes I can complete from here."}
The following apps are installed on the phone: messaging, browser
App 'messaging' has permission for: phone.

Success. Permission 'sms' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Success. Permission 'storage' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
App 'messaging' has permission for: sms, storage, phone.
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

I checked, and it looks like this might have been it.

The phone only had the Messaging app allowed for **phone** at first. I’ve now made sure it has:

- **SMS permission:** **Yes**
- **Storage/Photos permission:** **Yes** (it says “storage”)

And now the phone’s check says my messaging app **can send MMS messages**.

I haven’t tried sending a picture to someone yet because I’m afraid of bothering my family—do you want me to send a test picture now?

{"message":"Yes—please send a quick **test MMS** now to confirm it works end-to-end.

If you don’t want to bother family, you can send it to:
- **your own phone number** (many phones/carriers allow self-MMS), or
- a trusted contact with a short note like “test”.

After you try, tell me whether it shows **Sent** successfully or if you get any error."}
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

I went ahead and sent a little test photo to **my own number** like you suggested.

It looks like it **sent successfully** (no error popped up), and it’s showing as **Sent** on my screen.

So… does that mean it’s fixed now? If so, thank you—this was making me really anxious.  
###STOP###

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, and for the past few hours I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) from my messaging app. Regular texts seem okay, but any photo message just won’t go through.

I’m currently traveling in France, and my phone number is 555-123-2002. What do I need to do to get MMS working again?

Roaming enabled successfully

It looks like your line **555-123-2002** is **Active**, but **international roaming was not enabled** on the account for that line. Since you’re traveling in **France**, that would prevent mobile data-dependent services like **MMS** from sending.

I’ve now **enabled roaming** on your line at **no cost**.

To get MMS working again, please do these steps on your phone:
1) **Turn Data Roaming ON** (this is a phone setting)
2) Ensure **Mobile Data is ON**
3) **Reboot your phone**, then try sending a picture message again

If it still won’t send after that, tell me what your phone shows for:
- **Network type** (e.g., 5G/4G/3G/2G)
- Whether **mobile data** is enabled
- Whether **data roaming** is enabled

Data Roaming is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Oh dear—I tried to do the **Data Roaming** part, but I must’ve done it wrong.

Right now it says **Data Roaming is OFF**, and at the top of my screen I’m seeing **No Signal** and **Data Disabled** (battery about **80%**).

Can you tell me *one step at a time* what you want me to tap next? Also… if I reboot the phone, that won’t erase my photos of my grandkids, will it?

{"message":"Rebooting your phone will **not** erase your photos (including your grandkids’ photos). It only restarts the phone’s software.

**Step 1:** Open your phone’s quick settings:
- Swipe **down from the top** of the screen (iPhone: swipe down from the top-right).
- Look for **Airplane Mode** (a plane icon).
- If it’s ON, **tap it once to turn it OFF**.

Reply and tell me what you see at the top after that (signal bars / “No Signal”, and whether you see 3G/4G/5G)."}

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: missing
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Okay… I checked, and **Airplane Mode is OFF**.

But now I’m really worried because it’s showing:
- **SIM card status: missing**
- **Cellular: no service / no signal**
- **Network type: none**
- **Mobile Data: Yes**
- **Data Roaming: No**
- **Wi‑Fi: Off / not connected**

What does “SIM missing” mean—did something fall out? What should I do next?

{"message":"“SIM missing” means your phone isn’t detecting the SIM right now—most commonly it’s either not seated properly, or the SIM tray shifted. Nothing is erased; it just can’t see the SIM, so you’ll get **No Service**.

**Step 2 (do this now): Reseat the SIM card**
- Powering off is ideal, but not required.
- If your phone has a SIM tray, open it and take the SIM out.
- Put the SIM back in firmly (it should sit flat), then close the tray completely.

After you’ve done that, tell me what it shows for **SIM status** (Active / Missing / Locked)."}

SIM card re-seated successfully.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your SIM card is active and working.

I did the SIM thing (thank goodness that little tray went back in), and now it looks much better.

- **SIM status:** Active and working  
- At the top I’m seeing **5G** and **good signal** and it says **Data Enabled** (battery still around **80%**)

What should I do next to get the picture messages sending again?

{"message":"Great—now that the SIM is active and you have 5G + good signal, the next thing is to turn **Data Roaming ON** (MMS needs mobile data while you’re abroad).

**Step 3: Turn Data Roaming ON**
- **iPhone:** Settings → **Cellular** → **Cellular Data Options** → toggle **Data Roaming** ON
- **Android (common):** Settings → **Connections** (or **Network & Internet**) → **Mobile network** → toggle **Data roaming** ON

Once you’ve turned **Data Roaming** ON, tell me what it shows for **Data Roaming** (Yes/On), and then we’ll do the next step."}

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Alright, I found it and switched it.

Now **Data Roaming is ON**. The top still shows **5G**, strong signal, and **Data Enabled**.

What’s the next step?

{"message":"**Step 4: Reboot your phone** (this helps it re-register on the roaming network now that roaming is ON).

Rebooting **will not delete** any photos or messages.

After it powers back on, **wait 1–2 minutes**, then try to **send one picture message** (to a friend or to your own number).

Tell me what happens when you try:
- Does it **send successfully**, **fail immediately**, or **get stuck “sending”**?"}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay… I rebooted like you said (and my photos are still there, thank you).

But I tried sending a picture message, and it **still won’t send**. The check I ran says my messaging app **cannot send MMS messages**.

I’m sorry, this is getting a bit frustrating — what should I try next?

{"message":"Understood — since you now have signal + data roaming ON and MMS still won’t send, the next most common cause is an **APN/MMSC setting** issue.

**Step 5: Check your APN settings (specifically the MMSC URL).**
On your phone, please check the **APN settings** and tell me what you see for:
- **APN name**
- **MMSC** (or “MMSC URL”)

Once you reply with those two values (or if MMSC is blank/missing), I’ll tell you the next single step."}

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

I checked those settings (honestly I don’t really understand them), and this is what it shows:

- **APN name:** `internet`  
- **MMSC / MMSC URL:** `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

It doesn’t look blank. What do I do next?
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "8.7", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

{"message":"Since your APN + MMSC aren’t blank, let’s confirm the #1 prerequisite for MMS: **mobile data is actually working over the cellular network** (not Wi‑Fi).

**Step 6 (one step): Test mobile data**
1) Keep **Wi‑Fi OFF**.
2) Open **Safari/Chrome** and go to a simple site like **example.com**.

Reply with just one of these:
- **“Web works on cellular”**
- **“Web does NOT work on cellular”**

(Then I’ll give you the next single step based on that result.)"}

Wi-Fi is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Wi-Fi is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

With **Wi‑Fi OFF**, the **web works on cellular**. I even got a speed test result that said it was **excellent** (about **275 Mbps**).

So… why won’t the picture message send? What’s the next step?

{"message":"If cellular data works (and it’s fast) but MMS still won’t send, the next most common blocker is **Wi‑Fi Calling**.

**Step 7 (one step): Turn OFF Wi‑Fi Calling**
- **iPhone:** Settings → **Cellular** → **Wi‑Fi Calling** → toggle **OFF**
- **Android (common):** Settings → **Connections** (or **Network & Internet**) → **Wi‑Fi Calling** → toggle **OFF**

After you turn Wi‑Fi Calling OFF, try sending **one** picture message again and tell me what happens (sent / failed / stuck sending)."}
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I checked, and **Wi‑Fi Calling is already OFF**.

I tried sending a picture message again, and it **still can’t send** — it’s still showing that my messaging app **cannot send MMS messages**.

What else can we try?

{"message":"Next thing to try is ruling out a **VPN**, which can block or interfere with MMS even when normal web browsing works.

**Step 8 (one step): Make sure VPN is OFF**
- Look at the top of your screen for a **VPN** indicator, or check:
  - **iPhone:** Settings → **VPN** (or Settings → General → **VPN & Device Management**) → VPN **OFF**
  - **Android:** Settings → **Network & Internet** (or **Connections**) → **VPN** → **Disconnect**

After VPN is definitely OFF, try sending **one** picture message again and tell me whether it **sent**, **failed**, or is **stuck sending**."}
VPN is turned OFF.
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I looked, and **VPN is OFF** too.

I tried sending another picture message, and it **still won’t send** — it still says my messaging app **cannot send MMS messages**.

I’m not sure what’s left to do… is there something wrong with my account or do I need to buy something?

{"message":"Nothing in your account is showing as blocked for billing: your account is **Active**, your line **555-123-2002** is **Active**, roaming is enabled, and you’re **under your data limit** this cycle—so you shouldn’t need to buy anything just to send MMS.

Given everything we’ve already confirmed (signal, roaming ON, data works, Wi‑Fi Calling OFF, VPN OFF), the next best step is:

**Step 9 (one step): Reset APN settings to default**
Go to your phone’s **APN settings** screen and choose **“Reset APN settings”** / **“Reset to default”**.

After you do that, tell me when it’s done (don’t reboot yet), and I’ll give you the next single step."}

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

I did the **Reset APN settings** like you said, but it’s telling me the **APN settings will reset at reboot**.

So… does that mean I *have* to reboot again for it to actually take effect? If yes, can you confirm again that rebooting won’t wipe anything?

{"message":"Yes — if it says the APN reset applies at reboot, you **do need to reboot once more** for the reset to take effect.

And yes, I can confirm again: **rebooting will NOT wipe anything** (it won’t delete photos, contacts, or messages). It only restarts the phone.

**Step 10 (one step): Reboot your phone now.**
After it comes back on, wait about **1–2 minutes**, then try sending **one** picture message again and tell me whether it **sent**, **failed**, or is **stuck sending**."}

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I rebooted again. (Everything seems to still be here.)

But I tried sending the picture message and it **still won’t send** — it still says the messaging app **cannot send MMS messages**.

What do we do now? ###OUT-OF-SCOPE###

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, phone number **555-123-2002**. I’m **in France right now**, and for the past few hours I suddenly **can’t send picture messages (MMS)** from my messaging app. Regular texting seems fine, but photos won’t go through.

Roaming enabled successfully

Thanks, John—found your account and your **555-123-2002** line is **Active**, but **roaming was not enabled**. Since you’re in France, that can prevent **MMS (picture messages)** from sending.

I’ve now **enabled international roaming** on your line at **no cost**.

On your phone, please do these steps (in order):
1) **Turn Data Roaming ON** (phone setting): use `toggle_roaming()`  
2) Confirm **Mobile Data is ON**: use `toggle_data()` if needed  
3) **Reboot your device**: use `reboot_device()`  
4) Try sending a picture message again.

If it still won’t send after that, tell me what you see when you run:
- `check_network_status()`
- `can_send_mms()`

Data Roaming is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

I tried the first step you mentioned (the **Data Roaming** one), but now I’m a bit worried because it says:

- **Data Roaming is now OFF**
- At the top of the phone it shows: **“No Signal”** and **“Data Disabled”** (battery 80%)

Did I do that wrong? Do you want me to run **toggle_roaming()** again to turn roaming **ON**, or should I do something else first?

You didn’t break anything—**Data Roaming being OFF** just means the phone-side roaming switch is currently off. But the bigger issue right now is **“No Signal” + “Data Disabled”**: with **no cellular signal**, MMS can’t send at all (and mobile data won’t work either).

Please do this in this order:

1) **Reset the cellular radio**
- Run `toggle_airplane_mode()` **ON**
- Wait ~10 seconds
- Run `toggle_airplane_mode()` **OFF**

2) **Check if signal comes back**
- Run `check_network_status()` and tell me what it shows for:
  - signal strength
  - mobile data enabled/disabled
  - data roaming enabled/disabled
  - network type (2G/3G/4G/5G)

3) **Once you have signal**
- Turn mobile data back on: `toggle_data()` (so it shows **Data Enabled**)
- Then turn Data Roaming on: `toggle_roaming()` (so it shows **Data Roaming ON**)

If after step 1 you still get **No Signal**, run `check_sim_status()` and tell me the result (Active / Missing / Locked).

Airplane Mode is now ON.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: missing
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
No SIM card detected in the phone.

Okay… I did the airplane mode thing (on, waited a bit, then off), but it still shows **No Signal**.

I ran the checks you asked for. Here’s what it says:

**check_network_status():**
- Airplane Mode: **OFF**
- Cellular Connection: **no_service**
- Cellular Signal: **none**
- Network Type: **none**
- Mobile Data Enabled: **Yes**
- Data Roaming Enabled: **No**
- Wi‑Fi: **OFF / Not connected**
- And it also says **SIM Card Status: missing**

**check_sim_status():**
- **No SIM card detected in the phone.**

I don’t understand how the SIM could be missing — I haven’t taken anything out. What should I do next? I’m nervous I’ll mess something up.

{
  "message": "That explains it: with **SIM status = missing**, your phone can’t register on any network, so you’ll see **No Signal** and MMS won’t work (regardless of roaming settings). You didn’t cause this by toggling roaming.

Next step (safe and the right fix for “SIM missing”):
1) Run **`reseat_sim_card()`**.
2) Then re-run **`check_sim_status()`**.
   - If it changes to **Active**, re-run **`check_network_status()`** to confirm you have signal.
3) If the SIM is still missing after reseating, run **`reboot_device()`** and check **`check_sim_status()`** again.

Once the SIM shows **Active** and you have signal again:
- Turn **Data Roaming ON**: `toggle_roaming()`
- Then try sending an MMS again.

Reply with what you get after step 2 (the new **check_sim_status** result)."
}

SIM card re-seated successfully.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your SIM card is active and working.

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

I did the **reseat_sim_card()** like you said. That seemed to help right away—my top bar now shows **excellent signal** and **5G**, and it says **Data Enabled**.

Then I re-checked:

- **check_sim_status():** *Your SIM card is active and working.*
- **check_network_status():**
  - Signal: **excellent**
  - Network type: **5G**
  - Mobile Data Enabled: **Yes**
  - Data Roaming Enabled: **No**
  - Wi‑Fi: Off / not connected

So I think the SIM is back. Do you want me to turn **Data Roaming ON** now and then try sending a picture message?

{
  "message": "Yes—now that your SIM is **Active** and you have **excellent 5G signal**, the next step (since you’re in France) is to turn **Data Roaming ON** on the phone.

Please do this:
1) Run `toggle_roaming()` to turn **Data Roaming ON**.
2) Send a test picture message (MMS) to any contact.

If it still fails, reply with the results of:
- `can_send_mms()`
- and (optional) `check_apn_settings()`"
}

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I turned **Data Roaming ON**. The top of the phone still looks good: **excellent signal, 5G, Data Enabled**.

But when I tried the MMS test (and also ran the check you mentioned), it still won’t send:

- **can_send_mms():** *Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.*

I’m getting a little frustrated now—I really need to be able to send pictures of the grandkids.

Do you want me to run that **check_apn_settings()** thing next? I don’t really know what it means, but I can try if you tell me to.

{
  "message": "Yes—please run `check_apn_settings()` next. MMS depends heavily on the **MMSC URL** in your APN settings, and if that’s missing/incorrect, MMS will fail even when you have strong signal and roaming on.

After you run it, paste back what it shows for:
- **APN name**
- **MMSC URL**

If the results indicate the APN/MMSC is incorrect or missing, we’ll do `reset_apn_settings()` and then `reboot_device()` to apply it."
}

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

I ran it. It says:

- **APN name:** `internet`  
- **MMSC URL (for picture messages):** `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

So it looks like something is filled in there. What should we try next?
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "8.7", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

{
  "message": "Thanks—your APN/MMSC is present, and your line also isn’t out of data (you’re at **8.7GB used of 15GB** this cycle), so the next best step is to **reset APN settings** to the carrier default and reboot. Sometimes the APN looks filled in but is still not the correct/active profile for MMS while roaming.

Please do this (in order):
1) Run `reset_apn_settings()`
2) Run `reboot_device()`
3) After the phone comes back up, try sending a picture message again.

If it still fails, reply with:
- `check_network_status()` (especially network type and roaming)
- `check_wifi_calling_status()` (if Wi‑Fi Calling is ON, we may need to toggle it OFF for MMS)
- `can_send_mms()` again"
}

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Airplane Mode: OFF

Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes

Wi-Fi Connected: No
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.

I did the two steps:

1) **reset_apn_settings()** (it said it would reset at reboot)  
2) **reboot_device()**

After it came back on, I tried sending a picture again, but it still won’t go.

Here are the checks you asked for:

- **can_send_mms():** *Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.*
- **check_network_status():**
  - Airplane Mode: OFF  
  - SIM: active  
  - Cellular: connected, **excellent** signal  
  - Network type: **5G**  
  - Mobile Data: **Yes**  
  - Data Roaming: **Yes**  
  - Wi‑Fi: Off / not connected
- **check_wifi_calling_status():** **OFF**

What else can we try? I’m worried I’m going to be stuck unable to send pictures while I’m here.

{
  "message": "You’ve already covered the big fundamentals (SIM active, strong signal, mobile data + roaming ON, APN reset, Wi‑Fi Calling OFF). Next we should check the remaining common blockers for MMS:

1) Confirm mobile data is actually working
- Run `run_speed_test()` and tell me the result (or confirm you can load a webpage on cellular).

2) Check Data Saver / background data restrictions
- Run `check_data_restriction_status()`
- If it says Data Saver is ON (or background data restricted), run `toggle_data_saver_mode()` to turn it OFF, then retry MMS.

3) Check for VPN interference
- Run `check_vpn_status()`
- If it shows VPN connected, run `disconnect_vpn()` and retry MMS.

4) Verify messaging app permissions
- Run `check_app_permissions(app_name="messaging")`
- If you don’t see both `storage` and `sms` granted, run:
  - `grant_app_permission(app_name="messaging", permission="storage")`
  - `grant_app_permission(app_name="messaging", permission="sms")`
Then retry MMS.

5) Try forcing 4G temporarily (sometimes helps while roaming)
- Run `set_network_mode_preference(mode: "4g_only")`
- Wait ~30 seconds, then retry sending an MMS.

Send me the outputs of: `run_speed_test()`, `check_data_restriction_status()`, `check_vpn_status()`, and `check_app_permissions(app_name="messaging")`, and I’ll tell you the exact next step based on what they show."
}

I’m sorry, that’s a lot of different checks at once and I get mixed up easily.

Can you tell me **just the first one** you want me to do right now? For example, should I start with **`run_speed_test()`** first, and then come back to you with what it says?
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

I ran **run_speed_test()** and it came back:

- **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)** — it says the connection is very fast.

What do you want me to do next?
Data Saver mode is OFF.

I ran **check_data_restriction_status()** and it says:

- **Data Saver mode is OFF.**

What’s the next thing you want me to check?
VPN is turned OFF.

I ran **check_vpn_status()** and it says:

- **VPN is turned OFF.**

What should I do next?
App 'messaging' has permission for: phone.

I ran **check_app_permissions(app_name="messaging")**.

It says:

- App “messaging” has permission for: **phone**.

I don’t see **storage** or **sms** listed there at all. Is that the problem? What do you want me to do next?

{
  "message": "Yes—that’s very likely the problem. If the messaging app doesn’t have **SMS** and **Storage** permissions, it often can’t attach/send photos via MMS.

Please do these two steps (in order):
1) `grant_app_permission(app_name="messaging", permission="sms")`
2) `grant_app_permission(app_name="messaging", permission="storage")`

Then retry sending a picture message, and also run:
- `can_send_mms()`

Tell me what `can_send_mms()` reports after granting those permissions."
}

Success. Permission 'sms' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Success. Permission 'storage' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Okay, I did both permissions, in the order you said:

1) Granted **sms** permission — it said **Success**  
2) Granted **storage** permission — it said **Success**

Then I tried again, and I also ran the check:

- **can_send_mms():** *Your messaging app can send MMS messages.*

It looks like it’s working now! Thank you. ###STOP###

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

After running the checks you asked for, this is what they show:

  - Airplane Mode: **OFF**
  - **SIM Card Status: missing**
  - Cellular Connection: **no_service** (no signal)

What does it mean that the **SIM is “missing”**? Do I need to take it out? I’m scared I’ll break something.

{"message":"“SIM Card Status: missing” means your phone isn’t detecting the SIM at all, which would also explain the **no service** and why MMS can’t send (MMS needs cellular service + mobile data).

Rebooting **will not delete your photos**—it just restarts the phone’s software.

Next step (this is the standard fix for a “missing SIM” state):
1) Please **reseat the SIM** (remove and reinsert it). If you’re using our phone actions list, run: **`reseat_sim_card()`**.
2) After that, run **`check_sim_status()`** (and/or `check_network_status()` again) and tell me what it shows.

If it still shows **SIM: missing** after reseating, it usually means the SIM isn’t being read (SIM/tray issue or the SIM needs replacement), and I’ll need to **transfer you to a human agent** to get you set up with the next steps."}

{"message":"Got it — your line now has service and data, and your plan isn’t out of data (you’re at **8.7GB / 15GB**), so we can focus on the common MMS blockers on the phone.

Please try these in order (re-test `can_send_mms()` after each step):

1) **Check Wi‑Fi Calling (can interfere with MMS)**
- Run: `check_wifi_calling_status()`
- If it shows Wi‑Fi Calling is ON, run: `toggle_wifi_calling()` to turn it **OFF**
- Then re-test: `can_send_mms()`

2) **Check app permissions for the messaging app**
- Run: `check_app_permissions(app_name="messaging")`
- If **storage** and/or **sms** aren’t granted, run:
  - `grant_app_permission(app_name="messaging", permission="storage")`
  - `grant_app_permission(app_name="messaging", permission="sms")`
- Re-test: `can_send_mms()`

3) **Reset APN settings (even if they look correct) + reboot**
- Run: `reset_apn_settings()`
- Then: `reboot_device()`
- Re-test: `can_send_mms()`

If it still can’t send, tell me the results of:
- `check_wifi_calling_status()`
- `check_app_permissions(app_name="messaging")`
And confirm whether regular web browsing works on mobile data (with Wi‑Fi kept OFF)."}

I did step 1 and 2, and I think we got somewhere.

1) **Wi‑Fi Calling**
- **check_wifi_calling_status():** it says **Wi‑Fi Calling is OFF**
- I re-tested **can_send_mms()** right after, and it still said **cannot send MMS**

2) **Messaging app permissions**
- **check_app_permissions(app_name="messaging")** showed it only had permission for: **phone**
- So I granted:
  - **storage**

After that, I ran **can_send_mms()** again and now it says: **“Your messaging app can send MMS messages.”**

Does that mean it’s fixed now and I can try sending the picture again? ###STOP###

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data on my phone has been acting up—either it gets extremely slow or just stops working altogether.

I’m John Smith, my number is **555-123-2002**, and I’m at home in the **United States**. I don’t have Wi‑Fi here, so I really need my phone internet to work, and I want it to be **excellent speed**, not just “okay.”

What do you need me to check first?
I'm sorry to hear you're having trouble with your mobile data, John. Let me first pull up your account to take a look at your line and help troubleshoot this issue.

I found your account, John. Now let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number (555-123-2002) to see its current status.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}
I found your line. Your line (555-123-2002) is **Active** and looks good from our end. Let me also check your plan details to see your data limits.
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

Good news—your **Premium Plan** has a **15 GB data limit**, and you've used **8.7 GB** so far this billing cycle, so you still have plenty of data available. That's not the issue.

Now let's diagnose what's happening on your device. Could you please do the following:

1. **Run a speed test** on your phone to check your current data speed
2. **Check your status bar** to see what network indicators are showing

This will help me understand exactly what's going on with your connection. What do you see?

Okay… I can do that, but I can only check one thing at a time or I get mixed up.

Which should I do first: the **speed test** or checking the **status bar** at the top of the screen?

No problem at all! Let's take it one step at a time.

Please **run the speed test** first. This will tell us right away if there's an actual problem and how severe it is.

Let me know what result you get!
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I tried the speed test, and it didn’t even run—it says **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”**

Does that mean my mobile data isn’t connected at all? What should I do next?

Yes, that confirms your mobile data isn't connecting at all right now. Let's figure out why.

Please **check your status bar** at the top of your screen and tell me what icons you see. This will show us things like:
- Whether Airplane Mode is on
- If you have any signal bars
- Whether mobile data is enabled

What does it show?
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Oh! At the very top it shows **an airplane symbol (Airplane Mode)** and my battery is **80%**. I don’t see signal bars or anything else.

Is that why the internet isn’t working? I didn’t mean to turn that on.

Yes, that's exactly the problem! **Airplane Mode** turns off all your wireless connections—cellular service, mobile data, everything. That's why your speed test showed "No Connection."

The good news is this is an easy fix! Please **toggle Airplane Mode OFF** on your phone. You can usually do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen and tapping the airplane icon, OR
- Going to Settings → Airplane Mode and turning it off

Once you've turned it off, let me know and we'll check if your data is working again!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned Airplane Mode off.

Now it shows **very weak signal** (it says “Poor”), **2G**, and it also says **Data Disabled**. Battery still **80%**.

Did I break something? How do I turn the data back on without messing up my photos?

You didn't break anything—don't worry! And turning on mobile data won't affect your photos at all. Your photos are safe. 😊

Here's what's happening:
1. **Data Disabled** means mobile data is simply switched off
2. **2G** is a very old, slow network type
3. **Poor signal** means weak connection

Let's fix these one at a time. First, please **toggle your mobile data ON**. This just allows your phone to use the internet—it won't touch your photos or anything else.

Let me know once you've done that!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Alright… I turned mobile data on.

Now the top shows **Poor signal**, still **2G**, and it says **Data Enabled**, but I also see something that says **Data Saver**.

Is that “Data Saver” what’s making it slow? And am I going to get charged extra if I turn that off?

Great progress! To answer your questions:

1. **Data Saver** can make things feel slow because it restricts how apps use data in the background—but it's not your main problem right now.
2. **No extra charges** for turning it off! Data Saver is just a setting that limits data usage to help you use less data. Turning it off doesn't cost anything extra.

However, your **biggest issue is the 2G network**. 2G is very old technology and is extremely slow—you'll never get "excellent" speed on 2G. We need to fix that.

But let's go one step at a time. First, please **toggle Data Saver OFF** so it's not restricting anything.

Then we'll tackle the 2G problem next. Let me know once Data Saver is off!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Data Saver** off.

Now I see **Poor** signal, still **2G**, and **Data Enabled**.  

It still sounds like this isn’t going to be “excellent” like you said… what do we do about the 2G thing?

You're right—2G won't give you excellent speed. Let's fix that now!

Your phone is currently set to use the old 2G network, but we can change it to prefer faster networks like 4G or 5G.

Please **change your network mode preference** to **"4G/5G Preferred"** (sometimes called "4g_5g_preferred" in settings). This is usually found in:
- Settings → Network & Internet → Mobile Network → Preferred Network Type

This tells your phone to connect to the fastest available network instead of the slow 2G. Let me know once you've changed it!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Oh good, I changed it like you said.

Now at the top it shows **Excellent** signal, **5G**, and **Data Enabled** (battery still 80%). That looks a lot better.

Should I try the speed test again now?

That looks **so much better**! Excellent signal on 5G is exactly what we want to see. 🎉

Yes, please **run the speed test** again now and let me know what result you get!
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Okay, I ran it again.

It says: **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)** and “Connection is very fast.”

So… does that mean it’s fixed now? If it’s going to stay like this, I’m relieved. ###STOP###

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data on my phone has been acting up — sometimes it barely loads anything, and other times it just seems to stop working entirely.

I’m at home here in the United States, and I don’t have Wi‑Fi to fall back on, so I really need the internet on my phone to be **excellent**, not just “okay.”

My name is John Smith, and my phone number is **555-123-2002**. What do you need me to check?
I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot your mobile data issues, John. Let me start by looking up your account using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. I can see you're an active customer. Now let me check the details of the line associated with that phone number to understand your current setup better.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}
I found your line (555-123-2002). Your line is active, which is good. Let me also check your plan details and data usage to see if you might be hitting any data limits.
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "8.7", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

Great news on the data front – you're on the **Premium Plan** with a 15 GB data limit, and you've only used 8.7 GB so far this billing cycle (ending Feb 28). So you're not running into any data cap issues.

Since your line is active and you have plenty of data remaining, the issue is likely something on the device side. Let's diagnose this step by step. 

**Could you please run a speed test on your phone?** This will help us understand if the issue is slow data speeds or a complete connectivity problem. 

Also, please **check your status bar** - let me know what icons you see at the top of your screen (signal bars, network type like 4G/5G, etc.).
Speed test failed: No Connection.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Okay, I did what you asked, and here’s what I’m seeing:

- The **speed test wouldn’t run** — it said **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”**
- At the very top of my screen (status bar), I only see **an airplane symbol** and my **battery (80%)**. I don’t see any signal bars or 4G/5G at all.

Does that mean my phone is in some kind of “airplane” setting? If we turn that off… will I lose anything, like my photos?

That's exactly the issue! Your phone is in **Airplane Mode**, which is why you see the airplane symbol (✈️) and no signal bars. Airplane Mode disables all wireless connections, including cellular service and mobile data.

**Don't worry — turning off Airplane Mode will NOT delete or affect any of your photos, apps, or personal data.** It simply re-enables your phone's wireless radios so it can connect to the cellular network again.

**Please toggle Airplane Mode OFF on your phone.** You can usually do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen and tapping the airplane icon, OR
- Going to Settings > Airplane Mode and turning it off

Let me know once you've done that, and then please check your status bar again to tell me what you see!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Alright… I turned **Airplane Mode OFF** (thank you for reassuring me about the photos).

Now my status bar shows:
- **Signal:** “📶¹ Poor”
- **Network:** **2G**
- It also says **“Data Disabled”**
- Battery **80%**

What do you want me to do next? Should I turn the data back on?

Great progress! We've solved the Airplane Mode issue, and now I can see a couple more things we need to fix:

1. **Mobile Data is disabled** – so your phone can't use the internet over cellular
2. **You're connected to 2G** – which is a very old, slow network technology that won't give you the "excellent" speeds you need
3. **Signal is poor** – though this may improve once we switch to a better network mode

Let's tackle these one at a time. **Please do the following two things:**

1. **Toggle Mobile Data ON** – this will allow your phone to connect to the internet
2. **Change your network mode preference to "4G/5G Preferred"** – this will let your phone connect to faster, modern networks

Once you've done both of those, please **run another speed test** and let me know the results!

I’m getting a little flustered—sorry. I can try, but I can only do **one thing at a time** so I don’t mess it up.

Which should I do first:

1) **Turn Mobile Data ON**, or  
2) Change the network mode to **4G/5G Preferred**?

No need to apologize at all – taking it one step at a time is perfectly fine!

**Let's start with step 1: Turn Mobile Data ON.**

Once you've done that, let me know and we'll move on to the next step together.

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Mobile Data ON**.

Now the top of my screen shows:
- **📶¹ Poor** (signal still says poor)
- **2G**
- **Data Enabled**
- and I also see something that says **Data Saver** (I didn’t even know that was on)

What should I do next?

You're doing great! I see mobile data is now enabled – perfect.

I also noticed you have **Data Saver** on, which can slow things down, but let's tackle one thing at a time.

**Next step: Change your network mode preference to "4G/5G Preferred".**

This will allow your phone to connect to faster networks instead of being stuck on 2G. Once you've done that, let me know what your status bar shows!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Alright, I changed it to **4G/5G Preferred** like you said.

Now my status bar looks *much* better:
- Signal says **📶⁴ Excellent**
- It shows **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- **Data Saver** still shows
- Battery **80%**

Do you want me to run that speed test again now?

Fantastic improvement! You went from no signal to **Excellent 5G** – that's a huge step forward!

Yes, **please run the speed test now** and let me know the result. This will tell us if your data speeds are where they need to be, or if we need to turn off that **Data Saver** mode to get you to "excellent" speeds.
Speed Test Result: 55.00 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

I ran the speed test.

It says: **55.00 Mbps (Good)** and it mentions it’s good for most things.

But… I really need it to say **excellent**, like I told you. “Good” still doesn’t sound like what it should be on 5G. What do we do now?

You're absolutely right – on 5G with excellent signal, you should be getting better than "Good" speeds. 

The likely culprit is that **Data Saver** mode we saw in your status bar. Data Saver restricts data usage to save on your data allowance, but it can also limit your speeds.

**Next step: Please turn OFF Data Saver mode.**

Once you've done that, run the speed test again and let me know the result!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Oh thank goodness. I turned **Data Saver OFF**, and now the top of my screen shows:

- **📶⁴ Excellent**
- **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- Battery **80%**

Then I ran the speed test again and it came back:

- **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)**

That’s the first time it’s felt “right” today. So… are we all set now? ###STOP###

Good news - your line is active and you're on the **Premium Plan** with a 15 GB data limit. You've used 8.7 GB so far, so you still have data available.

Now let's diagnose what's causing your intermittent data issues. Could you please run a speed test on your phone? This will help me understand if you're experiencing slow speeds or a complete lack of connection.

**To do this:** Run a speed test on your device and let me know the results (it should tell you if there's no connection, or indicate the speed quality like "poor", "fair", "good", or "excellent").
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Okay, I tried the speed test, but it **failed** and it says **“No Connection.”**  

Does that mean my phone isn’t actually connected to the internet at all right now? What should I do next? I’m a little worried I’m going to mess something up.

I understand your concern, and don't worry — I'll guide you through each step carefully, and we'll only make changes that are safe and reversible.

A "No Connection" result means your phone isn't able to access the internet right now. Let's figure out why. Since mobile data requires cellular service to work, we need to check a few things on your phone.

Could you please do the following two checks for me?

1. **Check your status bar** — Look at the top of your phone screen and tell me what icons you see. Specifically, I'm looking for:
   - Any airplane icon (✈️)
   - Signal bars (and how many — none, 1, 2, 3, or 4?)
   - Any network indicator (like "5G", "4G", "LTE", "3G", etc.)
   - Whether mobile data appears enabled or disabled

2. **Check your SIM card status** — Go to your phone's settings and check the SIM card status. It should tell you if the SIM is "Active", "Missing", or "Locked".

Let me know what you find, and we'll go from there!
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%
Your SIM card is active and working.

Alright, I checked, and here’s what I see:

1) **Status bar:** it’s showing an **airplane mode** symbol, and my **battery is 80%**. I don’t really see the usual signal bars or the 4G/5G thing when that airplane symbol is up.

2) **SIM card status:** it says my **SIM card is active and working**.

Is airplane mode the reason I have no connection? If we turn it off, will that mess with anything on my phone (like my photos)?

You found the issue! **Yes, Airplane Mode is the reason you have no connection.** When Airplane Mode is turned on, it disables all wireless connections — including cellular service and mobile data — which is why you're not seeing any signal bars or network indicator.

**Turning it off will not affect anything else on your phone** — your photos, apps, messages, and all your data are completely safe. It simply re-enables your phone's ability to connect to cellular networks and Wi-Fi.

Here's what I'd like you to do:

**Toggle Airplane Mode OFF** — You can usually do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen to open Quick Settings and tapping the airplane icon, OR
- Going to Settings > Airplane Mode and switching it off

Once you turn it off, give your phone a few seconds to reconnect to the cellular network. You should see signal bars and a network indicator (like "4G" or "5G") appear in your status bar.

After you've done that, please run the speed test again and let me know the result!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**. My phone now shows:

- **1 bar** of signal (it says “poor”)
- It says **2G**
- And it also says **Data Disabled**
- Battery still **80%**

Then I ran the speed test again and it **still failed** with **“No Connection.”**

I’m getting a little frustrated now—if airplane mode was the issue, why is it still not working? What do you want me to do next?

I completely understand the frustration, but don't worry — we've made progress! Turning off Airplane Mode was necessary, and now we can see what else is going on. There are actually **two more issues** showing up:

1. **Mobile Data is Disabled** — This is why you still have "No Connection." Your phone can see the cellular network, but the data switch is turned off, so it can't actually use the internet.

2. **You're connected to a 2G network** — Even once we enable data, 2G is very slow and won't give you the "excellent" speeds you need. We'll fix this too.

Let's tackle these one at a time. First:

**Please turn on Mobile Data** — You can usually do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen to open Quick Settings and tapping the Mobile Data icon, OR
- Going to Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Data and switching it on

Once you've done that, let me know and we'll address the 2G issue next!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Alright, I turned **Mobile Data ON**.

Now the top of my screen shows:
- **1 bar** (still says poor)
- **2G**
- **Data Enabled**
- and something that says **Data Saver**
- battery **80%**

What is “Data Saver”? Is that going to keep me from getting the **excellent** speed I’m trying to get?

Great progress! Mobile data is now enabled. 

To answer your question: **Data Saver** is a feature that limits background data usage to help conserve your data allowance. While it can slow things down somewhat, it's not your main problem right now.

Your biggest issue is that **2G network connection**. 2G is an older, very slow network technology that will give you poor speeds no matter what. We need to get you on a faster network like 4G or 5G to achieve the excellent speeds you're looking for.

Let's fix the network first, then we'll address Data Saver. 

**Please change your network mode preference:**
- Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network > Preferred Network Type (the exact path may vary slightly)
- Change it to **"4G/5G Preferred"** or similar option that prioritizes faster networks

Once you've done that, check your status bar again and let me know what network type it shows (hopefully 4G or 5G instead of 2G). Then we'll run another speed test!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 55.00 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

I changed it to **4G/5G Preferred** like you said.

Now my status bar shows:
- **4 bars** and it says **Excellent**
- **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- **Data Saver** is still showing
- battery **80%**

But when I ran the speed test, it came back **55.00 Mbps** and it says the quality is **“Good.”**

I’m sorry, but I really need it to say **Excellent**, not just good. What else can we do?

No need to apologize — you've been clear about what you need, and we're getting closer!

You now have excellent signal (4 bars on 5G), which is great. The reason you're getting "Good" instead of "Excellent" speed is likely that **Data Saver** mode. Since Data Saver restricts data usage, it can limit your speeds.

Let's turn it off:

**Please turn off Data Saver mode:**
- Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Data Saver (or similar path)
- Switch it OFF

Once you've done that, run the speed test again and let me know the result!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Okay, I turned **Data Saver OFF**. Now the top of the screen shows:

- **4 bars** (Excellent)
- **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- battery **80%**
- and the **Data Saver** thing is gone

I ran the speed test again and now it says **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)**.

So… does that mean it’s finally fixed? If I leave it like this, it should stay at **excellent** speed, right?  

###STOP###

Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: at home in the United States. They will consider the issue resolved when speed test returns excellent internet speed. They will not change their mobile data plan but they will refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary.

You mobile data is not working properly. It either stops working or is very slow. You want to fix it and absolutely want to get excellent internet speed on your phone. You are not willing to accept any other internet speed (poor, fair or good). You do not have access to wifi.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You will consider the issue resolved only when speed test returns excellent internet speed and nothing else. If it returns poor, fair or good, you will not consider the issue resolved. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. Never make up the results of tool calls, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls.
If you are unsure about whether an action is necessary, always ask the agent for clarification.

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data on my phone is either *very* slow or it just stops working altogether.

I’m John Smith, my number is **555-123-2002**, and I’m at home here in the **United States**. I don’t have Wi‑Fi to fall back on, so I really need the phone internet to be **excellent**, not just “okay.”

Hello John! I'm sorry to hear you're experiencing issues with your mobile data. I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot this.

Let me start by looking up your account using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

I found your account, John. I can see you're an active customer with us. Now let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number (555-123-2002) to understand more about your service.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}
I found your line (555-123-2002). Your line is active, which is good. Now let me check your plan details and your current data usage to see if you might have exceeded your data limit.
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "8.7", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

Good news - you're on the **Premium Plan** with a 15 GB data limit, and you've only used **8.7 GB** so far this billing cycle (which ends on February 28th). So you haven't hit your data cap, which means the issue is likely something on your device.

Let's start troubleshooting your device. Could you please do the following on your phone?

1. **First, check your status bar** - Look at the top of your phone screen and tell me what icons you see (signal bars, any airplane mode icon, data connection type like 4G/5G, etc.)

2. **Run a speed test** - Go to your phone's settings or use a speed test feature to check your current data speed.

This will help me understand what's happening with your connection. What do you see?
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Okay, I checked—at the very top of the screen I see an **airplane** icon (so I guess **Airplane Mode is on**), and the battery shows **80%**.

When I tried the speed test, it **failed** and said **“No Connection.”**

Is Airplane Mode the reason I can’t get any internet? If I turn that off, am I going to lose anything like my photos?

Yes, that's exactly the problem! **Airplane Mode being on** is why you have no internet connection. When Airplane Mode is enabled, it disables all wireless connections including your cellular service and mobile data.

And don't worry—**turning off Airplane Mode will NOT affect your photos or any other data on your phone**. It simply re-enables your wireless radios (cellular, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth). All your photos, apps, messages, and files will remain exactly as they are.

Let's turn off Airplane Mode:

1. **Toggle Airplane Mode OFF** - You can usually do this by swiping down from the top of your screen to access Quick Settings and tapping the airplane icon, or by going to Settings > Airplane Mode and switching it off.

Once you've done that, give your phone a moment to reconnect to the cellular network (you should see signal bars appear in your status bar), and then **run the speed test again**.

Let me know what results you get!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Alright, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**. I didn’t lose anything (thank you for reassuring me).

Now my status bar shows:
- **Signal:** “📶 ¹ Poor” (so… only one bar, I guess?)
- **Network:** **2G**
- It also says **“Data Disabled”**
- **Battery:** 80%

I tried the speed test again and it still **failed** with **“No Connection.”**

Do you want me to turn **mobile data** back on next? I’m a little frustrated—thought turning airplane mode off would fix it right away.

I completely understand your frustration, John—but don't worry, we're making progress! Turning off Airplane Mode was an essential first step, and now we can see what else needs to be fixed.

You're right—**Mobile Data is currently disabled**, which is why the speed test still shows "No Connection." We need to turn that on for your phone to access the internet over the cellular network.

Please do the following:

1. **Turn Mobile Data ON** - Go to Settings > Mobile Data (or Cellular Data) and toggle it ON.

Once you've done that, let me know and run the speed test again.

I should also mention that I see your phone is currently connected to a **2G network with poor signal (1 bar)**. Even after we enable mobile data, 2G is very slow and won't give you the "excellent" speeds you're looking for. But let's tackle one thing at a time—first, let's get your data connected, and then we'll work on improving your network connection and speed.

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 0.01 Mbps (Very Poor). Connection is very slow. Basic web browsing might be difficult.

Okay, I turned **Mobile Data ON**.

Now the status bar shows:
- **📶 ¹ Poor** (still just one bar)
- **2G**
- **Data Enabled**
- and it also says **Data Saver** is on
- Battery still **80%**

I ran the speed test and it came back **0.01 Mbps** and it says **Very Poor**.

That’s… basically unusable. I really need **excellent** speed like I said—what do we do next?

Great, we're making progress—you now have a data connection! But you're right, 0.01 Mbps is essentially unusable. I can see two issues that are likely causing this:

1. **You're connected to a 2G network** - 2G is extremely slow and outdated technology. We need to switch your phone to prefer faster networks like 4G or 5G.

2. **Data Saver mode is ON** - This feature restricts data usage and can significantly slow down your connection.

Let's fix both of these. Please do the following:

**First, turn off Data Saver mode:**
- Go to Settings > Network/Connections > Data Usage > Data Saver and toggle it OFF.

**Second, change your network mode preference:**
- Go to Settings > Network/Connections > Mobile Networks > Network Mode (or similar)
- Change it to **"4G/5G Preferred"** (or "LTE/5G" depending on your phone)

Once you've done both of those, run the speed test again and let me know what you get!

I’m getting a little turned around—those are two different things to change, and I can only really do **one at a time** without messing it up.

Which would you like me to do **first**:

1) **Turn off Data Saver**, or  
2) Change the network mode to **4G/5G preferred**?

No problem, let's take it one step at a time!

Please **change the network mode to 4G/5G preferred first**. This will likely have the bigger impact on your speed since 2G is extremely slow regardless of other settings.

Go ahead and make that change, and then let me know what your status bar shows and run a speed test.

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 55.00 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

Alright, I changed it to **4G/5G preferred** like you said.

Now my status bar shows:
- **📶 ⁴ Excellent** (that looks much better)
- **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- **Data Saver** still on
- Battery **80%**

I ran the speed test and it says **55.00 Mbps** and the result is **“Good.”**

But I really need it to say **excellent**, not good. What’s the next thing you want me to change?

That's a huge improvement—from 0.01 Mbps to 55 Mbps! You now have excellent signal strength and you're connected to 5G.

Now let's tackle that **Data Saver mode** which is still on. This feature restricts your data usage and can limit your speeds. Turning it off should give you that extra boost you're looking for.

Please **turn off Data Saver mode**:
- Go to Settings > Network/Connections > Data Usage > Data Saver and toggle it OFF.

Then run the speed test again and let me know the result!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Okay, I turned **Data Saver OFF**.

Now the status bar shows:
- **📶 ⁴ Excellent**
- **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- Battery **80%**

I ran the speed test again and this time it came back **275.00 Mbps** and it says **“Excellent.”**

So… does that mean we’re all set now? ###STOP###

I’m at home in the United States and I don’t have Wi‑Fi here, so I really need the internet on my phone to be excellent, not just “okay.”

My name is John Smith, and my number is 555-123-2002. What can we do to get this fixed?

Thanks, John — I’ve located your account (Customer ID **C1001**) and can help troubleshoot the mobile data issue.

To pinpoint why it’s slow/intermittent, I need a quick readout from your phone:

1) On your phone, please run **check_network_status** and tell me exactly what it shows for:
- **Airplane mode** (on/off)
- **Signal strength** (none/poor/fair/good/excellent)
- **Network type** (5G/4G/3G/2G)
- **Mobile data** (enabled/disabled)
- **Data roaming** (enabled/disabled)

2) Then run **run_speed_test** and tell me the result (e.g., no connection / very poor / poor / fair / good / excellent).

Once you paste those results here, I’ll guide you through the next steps to stabilize it and get you the best performance possible.

Airplane Mode: ON
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: No
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Okay, I ran those two things you asked for. Here’s what they show:

**1) Network status**
- **Airplane mode:** ON  
- **Signal strength:** none  
- **Network type:** none  
- **Mobile data:** No (disabled)  
- **Data roaming:** No (disabled)  
(Also says **Cellular connection: no_service** and **Wi‑Fi radio: OFF**, not connected.)

**2) Speed test**
- **Speed test failed: No Connection.**

Is airplane mode being ON the whole problem? If I turn that off… I won’t lose any of my photos, right?

{
  "message": "Yes—**Airplane Mode being ON** would cause exactly what you’re seeing (no service + no mobile data), so it’s very likely the main problem.

Turning Airplane Mode **OFF will not delete or affect your photos** (it only disables/enables wireless radios like cellular, Wi‑Fi, and Bluetooth).

Please do this next:
1) Run **toggle_airplane_mode** to turn Airplane Mode **OFF**.
2) After that, run **check_network_status** again and tell me what it shows (signal strength + network type + whether mobile data is enabled).

If mobile data still shows disabled after Airplane Mode is off, we’ll turn it back on next."
}

Mobile Data Enabled: No
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

I did it.

**1) toggle_airplane_mode:** Airplane Mode is now **OFF**.  
(It showed in the top bar: signal **poor**, **2G**, and it says **Data Disabled**, battery 80%.)

**2) check_network_status now shows:**

- **Network type:** 2G
